Day 1.Employee A: Assess current warehouse capacity and identify expansion boundaries
Day 1.Employee B: Draft initial warehouse layout concept (rack placement, flow)
Day 1.Employee C: Conduct safety assessment of current warehouse (risks, hazards)
Day 1.Employee D: Document current inventory levels and categorize product types

Day 2.Employee A: Meet with construction vendor to discuss space requirements
Day 2.Employee B: Map loading/unloading zones and traffic flow improvements
Day 2.Employee C: Review fire safety regulations and emergency exit requirements
Day 2.Employee D: Archive existing stock records and prepare a reorganization list

Day 3.Employee A: Inspect available storage areas for potential expansion
Day 3.Employee B: Optimize shelving height and aisle spacing plan
Day 3.Employee C: Develop list of safety equipment needed
Day 3.Employee D: Identify slow-moving items to relocate to new storage zones

Day 4.Employee A: Approve contractor expansion draft and provide feedback
Day 4.Employee B: Plan new pallet racking system placement
Day 4.Employee C: Create updated emergency evacuation route draft
Day 4.Employee D: Label inventory sections for post-expansion relocation

Day 5.Employee A: Final site walkthrough and approval of expansion blueprint
Day 5.Employee C: Perform safety compliance checklist against expansion layout
Day 5.Employee D: Prepare updated inventory location map for staff training
